old_claim.note: Textiles & Weaving provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No preserved-textile dependency remains because the possible spindle whorls are about three millennia earlier than the graph's directly evidenced Catalhoyuk weaving node.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract dates the possible whorls to 12,000 years ago and reconstructs their use for spinning fibers into yarn.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The analyzed spinning technology belongs to the Late Natufian and predates the currently scoped preserved woven textiles at Catalhoyuk.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Spinning can supply many fiber products and cannot require a later surviving weaving example as its historical cause.

ontology_before: The earlier yarn-making tool depended on a later preserved woven-textile node.
ontology_after: Spindle whorls precede and can feed later textile weaving without a backward edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: drop_spindles no longer directly depends on textiles_weaving.
would_reject_if: The Nahal Ein-Gev II context were redated later than the Catalhoyuk textile contexts.
short_reason: The spinning evidence predates the scoped weaving evidence.
